elements.content:
JavaScript Starts in the Browser

A page loads, then your script enters the browser.

The Engine Executes It

The engine reads and runs the code step by step.

DOM = The Page Interface

JavaScript changes text, attributes, and styles through the DOM.

Browser APIs Do the Heavy Lifting

Built-in browser powers create elements, fetch data, and paint updates.

Events Wait Their Turn

Clicks and timers queue work; the loop runs it when the stack is free.

The 10-Second Mental Model

Code runs, changes the page, then events wake up more code.

- Code enters the JavaScript engine.
- DOM and browser APIs update the page.
- The event loop keeps interactions moving.
